title: Aurora, Bento, Draw-On, Springs & Dialog
description: CSS @property aurora glow cards, :has() bento reflow, SVG text draw-on, linear() spring buttons, native dialog animations.

drops.title: Aurora Glow Cards
drops.description: Rotating conic-gradient borders animated with @property hue — pure CSS aurora effect.
drops.concept: A conic-gradient border spins its hue continuously via the @property registered custom property, creating an aurora-like glow around cards. The ambient blur beneath is powered by the same hue property. No JS, no canvas — just a registered property and one @keyframes.
drops.source: Magic UI aurora card + aceternity.com glow patterns

interaction.mouse: Hover a card to reveal its aurora border

drops.title: Living Bento Grid
drops.description: :has() detects child hover and expands that card's grid-column span — zero JS, pure CSS reflow.
drops.concept: CSS :has() enables a parent to respond to descendant state. Here, the .bento grid container uses :has(.card:nth-child(N):hover) to change that card's grid-column value, causing the whole grid to reflow with a spring cubic-bezier animation. No JavaScript, no class toggling.
drops.source: 21st.dev bento grid + Vercel dashboard layout pattern

interaction.mouse: Hover any card to expand it

drops.title: SVG Text Draw-On
drops.description: stroke-dashoffset reveals letter paths, then fill fades in — paint-order keeps outlines crisp.
drops.concept: SVG text elements have a stroke-dasharray equal to the path length set to 2000. On hover, the dashoffset animates from 2000 to 0 in 1.4s, drawing each letter's outline. After 1s a fill-in keyframe fades in the solid fill color. The CSS paint-order: stroke fill property ensures the stroke doesn't bleed over the fill.
drops.source: SVG stroke-dashoffset draw-on technique — widely used on codrops.com and awwwards sites

interaction.mouse: Hover a word row to draw it on
interaction.click: Play All button animates all 5 words in sequence

drops.title: Spring Elastic Buttons
drops.description: CSS linear() spring easing approximates real physics — buttons squish and bounce without JS.
drops.concept: The CSS linear() function lets you define a custom easing curve with arbitrary control points. By sampling a spring physics equation, you get a lookup table that makes CSS transitions behave like a real spring (overshoot, oscillation, settle). Buttons scale to 0.88 on :active and spring back — no JavaScript animation frames needed.
drops.source: CSS linear() spring easing — web.dev + CSS WG + Jake Archibald's spring generator

interaction.mouse: Click any button to trigger the spring
interaction.click: Press and hold to see the squish

drops.title: Native Dialog Animations
drops.description: 5 native <dialog> elements each with a different @starting-style CSS entrance animation.
drops.concept: @starting-style is a new CSS at-rule that defines the initial style of an element before its first style calculation — enabling CSS transitions on elements entering the DOM or changing display state. Combined with the native <dialog> element and `display: allow-discrete` transitions, you get fully animated open/close without JS.
drops.source: Chrome developer blog on @starting-style + Open UI native dialog patterns

interaction.mouse: Click each button to open that dialog variant
interaction.click: Click backdrop or Close to dismiss
